attacking_style: Attacks on high-mountain finishes with 1-6km remaining, typically on final or penultimate climb. Shows preference for sustained efforts rather than explosive accelerations, often prioritizing positioning over stage wins.
team_tactics: FDJ historically struggles with positioning and protection in critical moments. Limited domestique support in mountains forces Pinot to self-select position. Team frequently caught chasing deficits rather than controlling.
terrain_preference: High-altitude sustained climbs suit best, particularly 6km+ finales where power-to-weight dominates. Exposed badly in crosswinds and technical positioning scenarios. Time trials remain persistent weakness despite marginal improvements.
vulnerability: Catastrophic positioning failures in nervous race situations (crosswinds, technical finishes). Final 200m sprint weakness when in breakaways. Injury-prone in critical race moments. Time trialing costs 50+ seconds routinely.
key_patterns: Crosswind positioning disasters cost 1:40+ even when starting high GC (2019 Tour evidence)
KOM jersey hunting from breakaways without GC commitment when out of contention (2023 Giro)
Loses breakaway sprints in final 200m consistently despite strong positioning (2 documented 2023 losses)
Attacks final/penultimate climbs 1-6km out with sustained efforts, rarely explosive accelerations
Injury withdrawals precisely before key mountain stages eliminate podium bids (2019 Tour 5th GC)
Time trial losses of 50+ seconds persist across years despite equipment upgrades
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows climbing resurgence after 2022 struggles, but early-season form inconsistent (dropped on Jaizkibel) and lack of GC ambition suggests transitioning to stage-hunting/classification role rather than overall contention
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 8
